name.value: The Altair 8800 simulator is part of the

value.value: The Altair 8800 simulator is part of the

name.value: family of simulators. Unlike a real Altair 8800 it features several enhancements:

value.value: family of simulators. Unlike a real Altair 8800 it features several enhancements:

name.value: Choice of processor (8080 CPU, Z80 CPU or 8086 CPU)

value.value: Choice of processor (8080 CPU, Z80 CPU or 8086 CPU)

name.value: Optional banked memory (16 banks with 64 Kbyte)

value.value: Optional banked memory (16 banks with 64 Kbyte)

name.value: MMU supporting ROM and memory mapped I/O

value.value: MMU supporting ROM and memory mapped I/O

name.value: Optional hard drive support for additional storage capacity

value.value: Optional hard drive support for additional storage capacity

name.value: Networking support via TCP/IP for client/server systems

value.value: Networking support via TCP/IP for client/server systems

name.value: Various devices for Northstar, Vector Graphic and CompuPro

value.value: Various devices for Northstar, Vector Graphic and CompuPro

name.value: Ability to set the clock speed for "real-time" simulation (useful for games)

value.value: Ability to set the clock speed for "real-time" simulation (useful for games)

name.value: Download the simulator with documentation and CP/M 2.2.

value.value: Download the simulator with documentation and CP/M 2.2.

name.value: Unpack the simulator to obtain altairz80.exe, the disk image (cpm2.dsk) and the command file (cpm2). Put all files into one folder. Start the simulator and type the command "do cpm2" at the sim> command prompt and CP/M is booted.

value.value: Unpack the simulator to obtain altairz80.exe, the disk image (cpm2.dsk) and the command file (cpm2). Put all files into one folder. Start the simulator and type the command "do cpm2" at the sim> command prompt and CP/M is booted.

name.value: Download the simulator with documentation and CP/M 2.2.

value.value: Download the simulator with documentation and CP/M 2.2.

name.value: Unpack the simulator to obtain AltairZ80.app, the disk image (cpm2.dsk) and the command file (cpm2). Put all files into one folder. Double click AltairZ80 to start the simulator and type the command "do cpm2" at the sim> command prompt and CP/M is booted. Alternatively you can drag the file "cpm2" onto the AltairZ80 icon (make sure that the path to cpm2 does not contain space characters).

value.value: Unpack the simulator to obtain AltairZ80.app, the disk image (cpm2.dsk) and the command file (cpm2). Put all files into one folder. Double click AltairZ80 to start the simulator and type the command "do cpm2" at the sim> command prompt and CP/M is booted. Alternatively you can drag the file "cpm2" onto the AltairZ80 icon (make sure that the path to cpm2 does not contain space characters).

name.value: Download the simulator (64-bit) with documentation and the CP/M disks. Alternatively download the 32-bit simulator version complete with documentation and the CP/M disks. Successfully tested on Ubuntu 22.04 (64-bit Desktop version).

value.value: Download the simulator (64-bit) with documentation and the CP/M disks. Alternatively download the 32-bit simulator version complete with documentation and the CP/M disks. Successfully tested on Ubuntu 22.04 (64-bit Desktop version).

name.value: Start a terminal or the console application and type "tar xzf altairz80l.tar.gz" and "./altairz80 cpm2" to boot CP/M.

value.value: Start a terminal or the console application and type "tar xzf altairz80l.tar.gz" and "./altairz80 cpm2" to boot CP/M.
